questions.q: In which city is Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus located?
questions.explanation: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus is the historic central railway station in the city of Mumbai.

questions.q: What type of building is Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus?
questions.explanation: It is a grand railway terminus, one of the busiest and most iconic railway stations in India.

questions.q: In which Indian state is Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus situated?
questions.explanation: Mumbai is the capital of Maharashtra, where the terminus stands.

questions.q: The terminus is named after which historical figure?
questions.explanation: It was renamed in honour of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, the 17th-century Maratha king.

questions.q: In which year was the building of Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus completed?
questions.explanation: The station building was completed in 1888 after about ten years of construction.

questions.q: What was the station originally called when it opened under British rule?
questions.explanation: It was originally named Victoria Terminus after Queen Victoria, and was popularly known as 'VT'.

questions.q: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus is recognised by which international body for its heritage value?
questions.explanation: The terminus was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2004.

questions.q: The dominant architectural style of Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus is best described as which of the following?
questions.explanation: The building is a celebrated example of Victorian Gothic Revival architecture blended with Indian elements.

questions.q: Which large feature crowns the central dome of the terminus?
options: A weather vane shaped like a ship
questions.explanation: The central dome is topped by a statue symbolising 'Progress', holding a torch and a wheel.

questions.q: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus serves as the headquarters of which railway zone?
questions.explanation: It is the headquarters of the Central Railway zone of Indian Railways.

questions.q: Who was the principal architect who designed Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus?
questions.explanation: The terminus was designed by the British architect Frederick William (F. W.) Stevens.

questions.q: In which year was the station officially renamed Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus (CST)?
questions.explanation: Victoria Terminus was renamed Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus in 1996.

questions.q: In 2017 the station's name was further expanded by adding which word?
questions.explanation: In 2017 'Maharaj' was added, making the full name Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT).

questions.q: Approximately how many years did construction of the terminus take?
questions.explanation: Construction began around 1878 and the building was completed in 1888, roughly a decade later.

questions.q: The terminus stands on the site of an older station bearing which name?
questions.explanation: It was built on the site of the earlier Bori Bunder station, an early terminus of the Great Indian Peninsula Railway.

questions.q: India's first passenger train, which ran in 1853, departed from the area where this terminus now stands. Where did that first train go?
questions.explanation: India's first passenger train ran in 1853 from Bori Bunder (Bombay) to Thane, near today's terminus.

questions.q: Which Indian decorative and craft tradition was blended into the building's otherwise European Gothic design?
questions.explanation: Stevens fused Victorian Gothic with Indo-Saracenic and traditional Indian decorative motifs.

questions.q: Sculpted animal figures of which two creatures flank the main entrance gates, symbolising Britain and India?
questions.explanation: A lion (representing Britain) and a tiger (representing India) guard the main entrance.

questions.q: Students of which Mumbai art school contributed to the decorative sculptural work on the terminus?
options: Sir J. J. School of Art
Government College of Fine Arts, Chennai
questions.explanation: Craftsmen and students associated with the Sir J. J. School of Art helped produce the building's rich ornamentation.

questions.q: Why was the original name 'Victoria Terminus' chosen?
questions.explanation: The station was named to honour Queen Victoria, then the reigning British monarch and Empress of India.

questions.q: Which devastating event of 26 November 2008 involved Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus?
questions.explanation: The terminus was one of the targets of the 26 November 2008 Mumbai terror attacks.

questions.q: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus handles both long-distance trains and which other major service?
questions.explanation: Besides long-distance trains, the terminus is a key hub for Mumbai's heavily used suburban local trains.

questions.q: In which year did UNESCO inscribe Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus as a World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: It was added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in 2004.

questions.q: The architectural style of the terminus is often specifically described as which variant of the Gothic Revival?
questions.explanation: Critics describe it as an Italianate or Venetian Gothic Revival design adapted to Indian conditions.

questions.q: Which railway company originally built and operated the terminus?
options: Bombay, Baroda and Central India Railway
questions.explanation: It was constructed as the headquarters of the Great Indian Peninsula Railway (GIPR).

questions.q: The female statue atop the central dome holds a torch in one hand and what in the other?
questions.explanation: The figure of 'Progress' holds a flaming torch in one hand and a spoked wheel in the other.

questions.q: Which type of stone forms much of the structural and ornamental fabric of the building?
options: Local sandstone, basalt and limestone with imported Italian marble accents
questions.explanation: The terminus uses local basalt, sandstone and limestone, accented with imported Italian marble and decorative stone.

questions.q: The terminus is widely cited as one of the first buildings in India to use which architectural achievement on a large scale?
options: A ribbed masonry dome over an octagonal drum
questions.explanation: Its large stone-ribbed dome rising over an octagonal drum was an ambitious feat for a building of its kind in India.

questions.q: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, after whom the station is named, was the founder of which empire?
questions.explanation: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj founded the Maratha Empire in the 17th century.

questions.q: Within the broader history of Indian railways, the terminus is significant because it marks the hub from which what began in 1853?
options: The first telegraph line in Asia
The first underground metro in India
The first electric tram in Bombay
The first passenger railway in India
questions.explanation: The area around the terminus was the starting point of India's first passenger railway service in 1853.
